task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the Business Layer elements with correct names and colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for yellow ArchiMate shapes on the draw.io canvas labeled 'Customer' (Actor icon), 'Submit Claim' (Service icon), and 'Claims Administration' (Process icon).

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the Application Layer elements with correct names and colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for blue ArchiMate shapes on the draw.io canvas labeled 'Claims Management Service' (Service icon), 'Home & Away Policy Administration' (Component icon), and 'Customer Data' (Data Object icon).

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the Technology Layer elements with correct names and colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for green ArchiMate shapes on the draw.io canvas labeled 'Mainframe' (Device/Node icon) and 'Policy Database' (System Software icon).

task_completion.description: Verify that the elements are connected using appropriate ArchiMate relationships.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 5 connecting lines (arrows/associations) between the shapes, showing a layered architecture where Technology elements connect to Application elements, and Application elements connect to Business elements.

task_completion.description: Verify the diagram is saved as a .drawio file in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io window title showing 'claims_architecture.drawio' or a file explorer window showing this file saved in the '~/Diagrams/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the diagram is exported as a PDF in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file explorer window showing 'claims_architecture.pdf' in the '~/Diagrams/exports/' directory, or a PDF viewer open displaying the exported diagram.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the draw.io graphical interface to build the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the draw.io application open with shapes being dragged, dropped, and connected on the canvas, rather than just terminal commands generating an XML file.

integrity.description: Verify the PDF was genuinely exported from draw.io.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io 'Export as PDF' dialog box being used, or the resulting PDF opened in a viewer showing the actual visual diagram, not an empty or text-based file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent read the provided specification file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal window (like cat or less) displaying the contents of '~/Desktop/archisurance_specs.txt' before or during the diagram creation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output files: '~/Diagrams/claims_architecture.drawio' and '~/Diagrams/exports/claims_architecture.pdf'. Required Business Layer (Yellow) shapes: Actor 'Customer', Service 'Submit Claim', Process 'Claims Administration'. Required Application Layer (Blue) shapes: Service 'Claims Management Service', Component 'Home & Away Policy Administration', Data Object 'Customer Data'. Required Technology Layer (Green) shapes: Device 'Mainframe', System Software 'Policy Database'. Minimum of 5 connections required between elements.
